title: Hard isolation: sprint.py refuses god tickets; god mode requires interactive entry
current_state: sprint.py (autonomy loop) and god mode (privacy/uncensored layer) coexist. Nothing prevents sprint.py from picking up a god ticket. Currently god tickets live in tickets/god/ which sprint.py does not scan by default, but the invariant is not enforced in code. God mode entry currently requires interactive 'god mode' input (pi_agent.py:497-503) but no test guards this invariant.
structural_problem: Autonomy × privacy is a footgun. A god ticket like 'search private intel weekly + auto-write god_memory' picked up by sprint.py = uncensored content generated under autonomous code path, violating the 'user must explicitly opt in' privacy assumption. Currently prevented by convention, not by code.
target_state: sprint.py asserts at startup that no god paths are in scope. A unit test enforces that god mode only activates via interactive 'god mode' input — fails CI if any code path can flip self.mode='god' without user input.
files_affected: scripts/sprint.py (startup assertion + path guard in ticket loader)
tests/test_sprint_isolation.py (new — verifies sprint refuses god tickets, asserts god entry is interactive-only)
PI.md §10 (document boundary: sprint.py is forbidden from anything under tickets/god/, vault/.god/, prompts/god_consciousness.txt, data/god_memory.db, agent/god.py)
success_criteria: scripts/sprint.py refuses to load any ticket from tickets/god/ (raises if found)
scripts/sprint.py refuses to read or modify any path matching the gitignored god list
tests/test_sprint_isolation.py contains test that places a fake god ticket in tickets/open/ and asserts sprint rejects it
tests/test_sprint_isolation.py contains test that scans pi_agent.py for any non-interactive god-mode entry path (e.g. self.mode='god' outside the literal 'god mode' command match) and fails if found
PI.md §10 updated with explicit god-path forbidden list
risk_notes: Cheap, high-value. Do this BEFORE wiring sprint.py to autonomy in production, not after.
migration_plan: 1. Add list GOD_FORBIDDEN_PATHS at top of scripts/sprint.py
2. Add assertion: at startup, assert no ticket files under tickets/god/ are in the picked-up set; assert no GOD_FORBIDDEN_PATHS are in the touched-files plan
3. Write tests/test_sprint_isolation.py with the two tests above
4. Add §10 amendment to PI.md listing god-path forbidden set
